opportunities.suggested_topic: Review US tech allocation: portfolio is significantly overweight relative to IPS target.
Address Gulf real estate overweight: allocation is 5.0 percentage points above IPS target.
Urgent review of FX policy compliance: SEK base currency exposure is 25 percentage points below the 60% IPS minimum.

weekend_changes.headline: Dubai Revises Real Estate Investor Visa Rules, Removes Minimum Property Value for Sole Owners
Abu Dhabi freezes rent increases on all property
Microsoft stock drops as FTC expands antitrust probe into cloud and AI licensing

three_nbas.title: Urgent: Remediate 25pp SEK Currency Floor Breach
three_nbas.rationale: The portfolio is in breach of the 60% SEK minimum exposure rule by 25 percentage points (currently 35%). This is a critical IPS violation requiring immediate action to mitigate unhedged currency risk. The plan is to reallocate approximately 120M SEK from non-SEK holdings into SEK-denominated assets.
three_nbas.projected_impact: Brings portfolio back into IPS compliance, significantly reducing foreign exchange risk from USD and other currencies against the client's base currency (SEK).
three_nbas.computation_trace: SEK base share = SEK 168,013,171 / SEK 479,857,590 = 35.01% (IPS floor=60% -> BREACH)

three_nbas.title: Execute Strategic Trim of Overweight Sleeves
three_nbas.rationale: Both US Tech and Gulf Real Estate are 5.00pp overweight. This action aligns with the client's stated concern about US tech valuations (supported by macro data on high P/E ratios) and the desire to reduce the size of the Gulf holdings. Proceeds from this trim should be used to address the SEK FX floor breach (NBA 1), and to fund the -5.00pp underweights in EU Fixed Income (as per client's green bond interest) and Alternatives. Trimming also resolves concentration breaches in Emirates REIT (8.34%) and Jabal Omar (6.67%).
three_nbas.projected_impact: Reduces concentration risk, captures gains, and generates ~48M SEK to fund the FX remediation and rebalance into underweight asset classes, aligning portfolio with IPS targets and client preferences.
three_nbas.computation_trace: Drift: US tech = +5.00pp; Gulf real estate = +5.00pp. Single-position breaches: Emirates REIT (8.34%), Jabal Omar Development Co. (6.67%). Underweights: EU fixed income = -5.00pp, Alternatives = -5.00pp.

three_nbas.title: Rebalance Concentrated Nordic Equity Positions
three_nbas.rationale: The Nordic Equity sleeve, while on target at 35% allocation, contains four single-stock positions that breach the 5% IPS limit: Investor AB (7.50%), Atlas Copco (6.67%), Volvo AB (5.83%), and Hexagon AB (5.42%). This presents idiosyncratic risk that should be managed. Propose a plan to trim these positions back towards the 5% limit and reallocate within the Nordic sleeve.
three_nbas.projected_impact: Reduces single-stock risk within the core Nordic equity portfolio, improving diversification without changing the overall asset allocation.
three_nbas.computation_trace: Single-position breaches: Investor AB ser. B (7.50%), Atlas Copco AB ser. A (6.67%), Volvo AB ser. B (5.83%), Hexagon AB ser. B (5.42%)

risk_flags.note: SEK exposure at 35.0% is 25pp below the 60% IPS minimum floor, representing a critical breach.
Multiple single-name holdings exceed the 5% AUM limit across Nordic Equity (e.g., Investor AB 7.5%), Gulf Real Estate (Emirates REIT 8.34%), and EU Fixed Income (German Bund 5.84%).
